guidelines.title: Avoid Anticholinergic Medications
guidelines.description: Highly anticholinergic medications increase risk of confusion, constipation, urinary retention, and falls in older adults.
action_steps: Review current medications for anticholinergic burden
Consider alternative medications with lower anticholinergic properties
If necessary, use lowest effective dose for shortest duration

guidelines.title: Avoid Benzodiazepines for Insomnia
guidelines.description: Benzodiazepines increase risk of cognitive impairment, delirium, falls, fractures, and motor vehicle accidents in older adults.
action_steps: If sleep medication necessary, consider ramelteon or low-dose doxepin

guidelines.title: Caution with NSAIDs
guidelines.description: NSAIDs increase risk of GI bleeding, renal failure, cardiovascular events, and exacerbation of heart failure.
action_steps: Use lowest effective dose for shortest duration
Consider PPI protection if GI risk factors present
Monitor renal function and blood pressure
Consider acetaminophen as first-line for pain

guidelines.title: Avoid Long-acting Sulfonylureas in Diabetes
guidelines.description: Glyburide and chlorpropamide cause prolonged hypoglycemia; prefer shorter-acting agents.
action_steps: Switch to glipizide or other shorter-acting agent
Consider metformin if renal function adequate
Monitor blood glucose closely during transition

guidelines.title: Multifactorial Fall Risk Assessment
guidelines.description: Comprehensive fall risk assessment including history of falls, gait/balance assessment, orthostatic hypotension, medication review, vision assessment, and environmental hazards.
guidelines.evidence_summary: Based on AGS Fall Prevention Guidelines 2023
action_steps: Conduct standardized gait assessment (Tinetti or Timed Up and Go)
Review medications for fall-risk increasing drugs

guidelines.title: Exercise Programs for Fall Prevention
guidelines.description: Group or individual exercise programs including gait, balance, and functional training to prevent falls.
guidelines.evidence_summary: Based on AGS Fall Prevention Guidelines 2023
action_steps: Balance and strength training at least 3x/week
Physical therapy referral for individualized program
Continue exercises indefinitely for sustained benefit

guidelines.title: Vitamin D Supplementation
guidelines.description: Vitamin D supplementation (800 IU/day) for adults aged 65+ with vitamin D deficiency or high fall risk.
guidelines.evidence_summary: Based on AGS Fall Prevention Guidelines 2023
action_steps: Supplement if <20 ng/mL or high fall risk
Monitor calcium levels if combined with calcium

guidelines.title: Home Safety Modifications
guidelines.description: Environmental modifications to reduce fall hazards, particularly for those with visual impairment or history of falls.
guidelines.evidence_summary: Based on AGS Fall Prevention Guidelines 2023

guidelines.title: ICOPE Cognitive Decline Screening
guidelines.description: Screen for cognitive decline using validated tools; intervene with cognitive stimulation, physical activity, and vascular risk factor management.

guidelines.title: ICOPE Locomotor Capacity Assessment
guidelines.description: Assess mobility using SPPB or gait speed; intervene with progressive resistance training and balance exercises.
action_steps: Measure usual gait speed over 4-6 meters

guidelines.title: ICOPE Malnutrition Screening
guidelines.description: Screen for malnutrition risk; intervene with nutritional counseling and protein supplementation if indicated.
action_steps: Vitamin D and calcium as needed

guidelines.title: ICOPE Vision Assessment
guidelines.description: Screen for vision impairment; intervene with refractive correction, cataract evaluation, and environmental modifications.
action_steps: Referral to ophthalmology for significant impairment
